steps.title: Apply the product rule for exponents
steps.content: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents. Group by variable: $(m^4 \cdot m^1)(q^4 \cdot q^5)(z^{-1} \cdot z^3)$.

steps.title: Add exponents for each variable
steps.content:
Add the exponents for each base:
  • $m$: $4 + 1 = 5$
  • $q$: $4 + 5 = 9$
  • $z$: $-1 + 3 = 2$
The equivalent expression is $m^5q^9z^2$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
